steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the given information
steps.content: The slope is $-54$, so $m=-54$. The line passes through $(0,0)$, so $f(0)=0$ and $b=0$. Therefore $$f(x)=-54x.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $f(x)=-54x$ matches choice A. The other choices have slopes $-36$, $-18$, and $1$, so they cannot match the given slope. Choice A is correct.
